title: Tunisia's Possible Lineup To Face Sweden In The 2026 FIFA World Cup
original_text:
The Tunisia national team enters another World Cup with the intention of making history once again. The Carthage Eagles arrive at the tournament motivated to compete on the international stage, but also with the need to rebuild confidence after a preparation period with some negative results.

The team led by Sabri Lamouchi is not going through its best moment. The coach himself has shown his dissatisfaction with the team's collective performance and with some mistakes that have appeared in recent matches. For Tunisia , the World Cup will not be the time to continue testing different options: the opening match against Sweden represents an opportunity to show a more solid and competitive version.

In addition, the match against the Swedes carries even greater importance because both teams arrive with some doubts. A positive result in the first game could completely change the group's outlook and give Tunisia the boost needed to fight for qualification.

The African national team arrives with doubts after its latest preparation matches. The defeats against Austria by 1-0 and the heavy loss against Belgium by 5-0 raised concerns within the squad. After that match, Lamouchi admitted his frustration with his players' performance and the lack of answers during certain moments of the game.

However, the coaching staff knows there is no longer much room for major changes. The next challenge will take place in Monterrey against Sweden , a team that also arrives with questions and could leave spaces if it fails to control the match.

The idea for Tunisia will be to compete through defensive organization, trying to withstand Sweden's moments of dominance and take advantage of the speed of its attacking players during transitions.

In attack, the team could line up in a 4-3-3 system, looking for width on the wings and movement in the offensive area. When losing possession, Rani Khedira could drop deeper to help create a more compact structure, turning the system into a 4-2-3-1 with Hannibal Mejbri taking a bigger role as the link between midfield and the forwards.

For Tunisia , the objective is clear: find stability, reduce mistakes, and secure a result that can change the team's momentum before facing the rest of the World Cup .

Follow all the coverage of the FIFA World Cup 2026 on beIN SPORTS.
